brief: Out of 1,271 scanned parcels, 921 qualified and 918 pencil, with top opportunities heavily concentrated in Brooklyn R6 and R7 sites leveraging a 485-x tax exemption and Section 8 VPS rents overlay. High-yielding Brooklyn opportunities offer substantial headroom relative to land comp estimates ($439 PSF in BK), while secondary options in the Bronx ($324 PSF) rely on competitive ELLA and 4% LIHTC subsidy stacks. Because all top deals are city-owned, acquisition relies on public disposition processes rather than direct private market purchases.

targets.play: Develop 10 units under 485-x with Section 8 VPS rents to capture a top-tier 31.8% cash-on-cash yield in an R7-2 zone.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $569,141 versus a walk-away max of $3,202,693, leaving $2,633,552 in valuation headroom.
targets.risk: Navigating the HPD disposition process for city-owned land and verifying site-specific R7-2 zoning constraints.

targets.play: Deliver a 14-unit R7A project using the 485-x tax exemption and Section 8 VPS rents targeting an 18.3% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $690,612 against a $2,337,060 walk-away threshold, yielding $1,646,448 in headroom.
targets.risk: HPD disposition process timelines and site condition diligence on city-owned property.

targets.play: Build 12 units in an R7A zone via 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents to generate a 16.9% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $591,738 compared to a $1,850,943 walk-away land cap, providing $1,259,205 in headroom.
targets.risk: HPD disposition requirements and verifying zoning bulk controls.

targets.play: Advance a 10-unit Bronx program play utilizing an ELLA / 4% LIHTC stack to earn a $333,039 developer fee.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $591,182 against a walk-away land value of $57,379, requiring city land write-down or full subsidy backing to pencil.
targets.risk: Subsidy competitiveness for program awards and HPD disposition approval.

process_note: Immediate next steps are to verify zoning capacity, inspect site conditions, and monitor HPD/EDC RFP pipelines for city-owned site disposition timelines.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
